review_notes: The functions provided are applicable to Kubernetes and align with the FedRAMP CM-7 control on least functionality. The naming conventions are mostly followed correctly, and the functions map to real Kubernetes security controls. However, the function 'k8s_pod_security_standard_restricted' could be more descriptive by specifying the exact security standard being enforced, such as 'restricted' or 'baseline'.
title: Least Functionality
step1_reasoning: The FedRAMP CM-7 control focuses on ensuring least functionality, which in Kubernetes can be mapped to several areas: NetworkPolicies to restrict ingress and egress traffic, RBAC to enforce least privilege access, and Pod Security Standards to limit pod capabilities. These controls collectively ensure that Kubernetes environments are configured to minimize unnecessary access and functionality, aligning with the principle of least functionality.
